children.heading: Authorization
children.label: a Authorization
children.text: The Secretary is authorized to establish in the Department the position of Under Secretary of Agriculture for Research, Education, and Economics (referred to in this section as the “Under Secretary”).

children.heading: Confirmation required
children.label: b Confirmation required
children.text: The Under Secretary shall be appointed by the President, by and with the advice and consent of the Senate, from among distinguished scientists with specialized training or significant experience in agricultural research, education, and economics.
hold the title of Chief Scientist of the Department; and
be responsible for the coordination of the research, education, and extension activities of the Department.

children.heading: Principal function
children.label: 1 Principal function
children.text: The Secretary shall delegate to the Under Secretary those functions and duties under the jurisdiction of the Department that relate to research, education, and economics.

children.label: A
children.text: identify, address, and prioritize current and emerging agricultural research, education, and extension needs (including funding);

children.label: i
children.text: across disciplines, agencies, and institutions; and

children.label: ii
children.text: among applicable participants, grantees, and beneficiaries;

children.label: C
children.text: promote the collaborative use of all agricultural research, education, and extension resources from the local, State, tribal, regional, national, and international levels to address priority needs; and

children.label: D
children.text: foster communication among agricultural research, education, and extension beneficiaries, including the public, to ensure the delivery of agricultural research, education, and extension knowledge.

children.heading: Additional functions
children.label: 3 Additional functions
children.text: The Under Secretary shall perform such other functions and duties as may be required by law or prescribed by the Secretary.

children.heading: Office of the Chief Scientist
children.label: e Office of the Chief Scientist

children.heading: Establishment
children.label: 1 Establishment
children.text: The Under Secretary shall organize within the office of the Under Secretary 6 Divisions, to be known collectively as the “Office of the Chief Scientist”, which shall coordinate the research programs and activities of the Department.

children.label: A
children.text: Renewable energy, natural resources, and environment.

children.label: B
children.text: Food safety, nutrition, and health.

children.label: C
children.text: Plant health and production and plant products.

children.label: D
children.text: Animal health and production and animal products.

children.label: E
children.text: Agricultural systems and technology.

children.label: F
children.text: Agricultural economics and rural communities.

children.label: I
children.text: the provisions of title 5 governing appointments in the competitive service;

children.label: II
children.text: the provisions of subchapter I of chapter 35 of title 5 relating to retention preference; and

children.label: III
children.text: the provisions of chapter 51 and subchapter III of chapter 53 of title 5 relating to classification and General Schedule pay rates;

children.label: ii
children.text: by detail, notwithstanding any Act making appropriations for the Department of Agriculture, whether enacted before, on, or after the date of enactment of this paragraph, requiring reimbursement for those details unless the appropriation Act specifically refers to this subsection and specifically includes these details;

children.label: iii
children.text: by reassignment or transfer from any other civil service position; and

children.label: iv
children.text: by an assignment under subchapter VI of chapter 33 of title 5.

children.label: i
children.text: promotes leadership and professional development;

children.label: ii
children.text: enables personnel to interact with other agencies of the Department; and

children.label: iii
children.text: maximizes the ability of the Under Secretary to allow for rotations of Department personnel into the position of Division Chief.

children.heading: Term of service
children.label: C Term of service
children.text: Notwithstanding title 5, the maximum length of service for an individual selected as a Division Chief under clauses (i) and (iii) of subparagraph (A) shall be for not less than 3 years.

children.label: i
children.text: conducted exemplary research, education, or extension in the field of agriculture or forestry; and

children.label: ii
children.text: earned an advanced degree at an institution of higher education (as defined in ).section 1001 of title 20

children.label: i
children.text: assist the Under Secretary in identifying and addressing emerging agricultural research, education, and extension needs;

children.label: ii
children.text: assist the Under Secretary in identifying and prioritizing Department-wide agricultural research, education, and extension needs, including funding;

children.label: iii
children.text: assess the strategic workforce needs of the research, education, and extension functions of the Department, and develop strategic workforce plans to ensure that existing and future workforce needs are met;

children.label: iv
children.text: communicate with research, education, and extension beneficiaries, including the public, and representatives of the research, education, and extension system, including the National Agricultural Research, Extension, Education, and Economics Advisory Board, to promote the benefits of agricultural research, education, and extension;

children.label: v
children.text: assist the Under Secretary in preparing and implementing the roadmap for agricultural research, education, and extension, as described in ; andsection 7614a of this title

children.label: vi
children.text: perform such other duties as the Under Secretary may determine.

children.label: A
children.text: to oversee implementation, training, and compliance with the scientific integrity policy of the Department;

children.label: i
children.text: to integrate strategic program planning and evaluation functions across the programs of the Department; and

children.label: ii
children.text: to help prepare the annual report to Congress on the relevance and adequacy of programs under the jurisdiction of the Under Secretary;

children.label: C
children.text: to assist the Chief Scientist in coordinating the international engagements of the Department with the Department of State and other international agencies and offices of the Federal Government; and

children.label: D
children.text: to oversee other duties as may be required by Federal law or Department policy.

children.heading: Authorization of appropriations
children.label: i Authorization of appropriations
children.text: There is authorized to be appropriated such sums as are necessary to fund the costs of Division personnel.

children.heading: Additional funding
children.label: ii Additional funding
children.text: In addition to amounts made available under clause (i), notwithstanding any Act making appropriations for the Department of Agriculture, whether enacted before, on, or after the date of enactment of this paragraph unless the appropriation Act specifically refers to this subsection and specifically includes the administration of funds under this section, the Secretary may transfer funds made available to an agency in the research, education, and economics mission area to fund the costs of Division personnel.

children.label: i
children.text: the Under Secretary shall minimize the number of full-time equivalent positions in the Divisions; and

children.label: ii
children.text: at no time shall the aggregate number of staff for all Divisions exceed 30 full-time equivalent positions.

children.label: i
children.text: promotes leadership and professional development;

children.label: ii
children.text: enables personnel to interact with other agencies of the Department; and

children.label: iii
children.text: provides strong staff continuity to the Office of the Chief Scientist.

children.heading: Organization
children.label: 6 Organization
children.text: The Under Secretary shall integrate leadership functions of the national program staff of the research agencies into the Office of the Chief Scientist in such form as is required to ensure that administrative duplication does not occur.

children.heading: National Institute of Food and Agriculture
children.label: f National Institute of Food and Agriculture

children.heading: Advisory Board
children.label: A Advisory Board
children.text: The term “Advisory Board” means the National Agricultural Research, Extension, Education, and Economics Advisory Board established under section 1408 of the National Agricultural Research, Extension, and Teaching Policy Act of 1977 ().7 U.S.C. 3123

children.heading: Applied research
children.label: B Applied research
children.text: The term “applied research” means research that includes expansion of the findings of fundamental research to uncover practical ways in which new knowledge can be advanced to benefit individuals and society.

children.label: i
children.text: Each program providing funding to any of the 1994 Institutions under sections 533, 534(a), and 535 of the Equity in Educational Land-Grant Status Act of 1994 ( note; ).7 U.S.C. 301Public Law 103–382

children.label: ii
children.text: The program established under section 536 of the Equity in Educational Land-Grant Status Act of 1994 ( note; ) providing research grants for 1994 Institutions.7 U.S.C. 301Public Law 103–382

children.label: iii
children.text: Each program established under subsections (b) and (c) of .section 343 of this title

children.label: iv
children.text: Each program established under the Hatch Act of 1887 ( et seq.).7 U.S.C. 361a

children.label: v
children.text: Each program established under section 1417(b) of the National Agricultural Research, Extension, and Teaching Policy Act of 1977 ().7 U.S.C. 3152(b)

children.label: vi
children.text: The animal health and disease research program established under subtitle E of the National Agricultural Research, Extension, and Teaching Policy Act of 1977 ( et seq.) except for the competitive grant program under section 1433(b) [].7 U.S.C. 319117 U.S.C. 3195(b)So in original. Probably should be followed by a comma.1

children.label: vii
children.text: Each extension program available to 1890 Institutions established under section 1444 of the National Agricultural Research, Extension, and Teaching Policy Act of 1977 ().7 U.S.C. 3221

children.label: viii
children.text: The program established under section 1445 of the National Agricultural Research, Extension, and Teaching Policy Act of 1977 ().7 U.S.C. 3222

children.label: ix
children.text: The program providing grants to upgrade agricultural and food sciences facilities at 1890 Institutions established under section 1447 of the National Agricultural Research, Extension, and Teaching Policy Act of 1977 ().7 U.S.C. 3222b

children.label: x
children.text: The program providing distance education grants for insular areas established under section 1490 of the National Agricultural Research, Extension, and Teaching Policy Act of 1977 ().7 U.S.C. 3362

children.label: xi
children.text: The program providing resident instruction grants for insular areas established under section 1491 of the National Agricultural Research, Extension, and Teaching Policy Act of 1977 ().7 U.S.C. 3363

children.label: xii
children.text: Each research and development and related program established under (commonly known as the “McIntire-Stennis Cooperative Forestry Act”) ( et seq.).Public Law 87–78816 U.S.C. 582a

children.label: xiii
children.text: Each program established under the Renewable Resources Extension Act of 1978 ( et seq.).16 U.S.C. 1671

children.label: xiv
children.text: Each program providing funding to Hispanic-serving agricultural colleges and universities under section 1456 of the National Agricultural Research, Extension, and Teaching Policy Act of 1977 [].7 U.S.C. 3243

children.label: xv
children.text: The program providing capacity grants to NLGCA Institutions under section 1473F of the National Agricultural Research, Extension, and Teaching Policy Act of 1977 [].7 U.S.C. 3319i

children.label: xvi
children.text: Other programs that are capacity and infrastructure programs, as determined by the Secretary.

children.label: i
children.text: The Agriculture and Food Research Initiative established under .section 3157(b) of this title

children.label: ii
children.text: The program providing competitive grants for risk management education established under .section 1524(a) of this title

children.label: iii
children.text: The program providing community food project competitive grants established under .section 2034 of this title

children.label: iv
children.text: The beginning farmer and rancher development grant program established under subsection (d) of .section 2279 of this title

children.label: v
children.text: The program providing grants under section 1417(j) of the National Agricultural Research, Extension, and Teaching Policy Act of 1977 ().7 U.S.C. 3152(j)

children.label: vi
children.text: The program providing grants for Hispanic-serving institutions established under section 1455 of the National Agricultural Research, Extension, and Teaching Policy Act of 1977 ().7 U.S.C. 3241

children.label: vii
children.text: The program providing competitive grants for international agricultural science and education programs under section 1459A of the National Agricultural Research, Extension, and Teaching Policy Act of 1977 ().7 U.S.C. 3292b

children.label: viii
children.text: The research and extension projects carried out under .section 5811 of this title

children.label: ix
children.text: The organic agriculture research and extension initiative established under .section 5925b of this title

children.label: x
children.text: The specialty crop research initiative under .section 7632 of this title

children.label: xi
children.text: The research, extension, and education programs authorized by relating to the competitiveness, viability and sustainability of small- and medium-sized dairy, livestock, and poultry operations.section 7627 of this title

children.label: xii
children.text: Other programs that are competitive programs, as determined by the Secretary.

children.heading: Director
children.label: E Director
children.text: The term “Director” means the Director of the Institute.

children.label: i
children.text: increases knowledge or understanding of the fundamental aspects of phenomena and has the potential for broad application; and

children.label: ii
children.text: has an effect on agriculture, food, nutrition, or the environment.

children.heading: Institute
children.label: G Institute
children.text: The term “Institute” means the National Institute of Food and Agriculture established by paragraph (2)(A).

children.heading: Establishment of National Institute of Food and Agriculture
children.label: 2 Establishment of National Institute of Food and Agriculture

children.heading: Establishment
children.label: A Establishment
children.text: The Secretary shall establish within the Department an agency to be known as the “National Institute of Food and Agriculture”.

children.label: i
children.text: the capacity and infrastructure programs;

children.label: ii
children.text: the competitive programs;

children.label: iii
children.text: the research, education, economic, cooperative State research programs, cooperative extension and education programs, international programs, and other functions and authorities delegated by the Under Secretary to the Administrator of the Cooperative State Research, Education, and Extension Service pursuant to section 2.66 of title 7, Code of Federal Regulations (or successor regulations); and

children.label: iv
children.text: any and all other authorities administered by the Administrator of the Cooperative State Research, Education, and Extension Service.

children.label: i
children.text: a distinguished scientist; and

children.label: ii
children.text: appointed by the President.

children.heading: Supervision
children.label: B Supervision
children.text: The Director shall report directly to the Secretary, or the designee of the Secretary.

children.label: i
children.text: serve for a 6-year term, subject to reappointment for an additional 6-year term;

children.label: ii
children.text: periodically report to the Secretary, or the designee of the Secretary, with respect to activities carried out by the Institute; and

children.label: I
children.text: research of the Institute is relevant to agriculture in the United States and otherwise serves the national interest; and

children.label: II
children.text: the research of the Institute supplements and enhances, and does not supplant, research conducted or funded by other Federal agencies.

children.heading: Compensation
children.label: D Compensation
children.text: The Director shall receive basic pay at a rate not to exceed the maximum amount of compensation payable to a member of the Senior Executive Service under subsection (b) of , except that the certification requirement in that subsection shall not apply to the compensation of the Director.section 5382 of title 5

children.heading: Authority and responsibilities of Director
children.label: E Authority and responsibilities of Director
i
children.text: exercise all of the authority provided to the Institute by this subsection;

children.label: ii
children.text: formulate and administer programs in accordance with policies adopted by the Institute, in coordination with the Under Secretary;

children.label: iii
children.text: establish offices within the Institute;

children.label: iv
children.text: establish procedures for the provision and administration of grants by the Institute; and

children.label: v
children.text: consult regularly with the Advisory Board.

children.heading: Regulations
children.label: 4 Regulations
children.text: The Institute shall have such authority as is necessary to carry out this subsection, including the authority to promulgate such regulations as the Institute considers to be necessary for governance of operations, organization, and personnel.

children.heading: In general
children.label: A In general
children.text: The Director shall organize offices and functions within the Institute to administer fundamental and applied research and extension and education programs.

children.heading: Research priorities
children.label: B Research priorities
children.text: The Director shall ensure the research priorities established by the Under Secretary through the Office of the Chief Scientist are carried out by the offices and functions of the Institute, where applicable.

children.label: i
children.text: determine an appropriate balance between fundamental and applied research programs and functions to ensure future research needs are met; and

children.label: ii
children.text: designate staff, as appropriate, to assist in carrying out this subparagraph.

children.label: i
children.text: promote the use and growth of grants awarded through a competitive process; and

children.label: ii
children.text: designate staff, as appropriate, to assist in carrying out this subparagraph.

children.heading: Coordination
children.label: E Coordination
children.text: The Director shall ensure that the offices and functions established under subparagraph (A) are effectively coordinated for maximum efficiency.

children.heading: In general
children.label: A In general
children.text: In addition to funds otherwise appropriated to carry out each program administered by the Institute, there are authorized to be appropriated such sums as are necessary to carry out this subsection for each fiscal year.

children.heading: Allocation
children.label: B Allocation
children.text: Funding made available under subparagraph (A) shall be allocated according to recommendations contained in the roadmap described in .section 7614a of this title
